papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than cognition in human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It develops a speech-based digital biomarker for dementia but uses neither an interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, nor a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working-memory-like behavior inside generative video diffusion models.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: It proposes commercial games as interactive executive-function paradigms and makes eye tracking and behavioral timing central to its observational methodology.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: It analyzes sequential Digit Span behavior and pupil dynamics as cognitive-load biomarkers, while its EEG analysis is feature-based rather than clearly frequency-domain or connectivity-focused.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: Excluded because it concerns video generation and KV-cache memory in a machine-learning model.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It addresses human cognitive-load monitoring with EEG, but the summary specifies learned representations and classification rather than an interactive task or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: It uses a working-memory task with human participants, but EEG is reduced to band-power and generic descriptors and the synthetic HRV model is not a generative model of cognition.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It includes an active working-memory paradigm, but its EEG contribution is limited to spectral and generic features used as regression targets for ECG.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: It combines interactive sustained-attention and working-memory training with eye tracking to adapt task difficulty in real time from human cognitive-load estimates.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: It analyzes beta-phase EEG connectivity during active recall and sequence working-memory tasks, directly aligning task behavior with network-level neural biomarkers.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: Excluded because the neural validation involves macaque data rather than an exclusively human-participant study population.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: It combines a working-memory task with EEG power, connectivity, and phase-amplitude coupling analyses to derive an individual sleep-quality biomarker.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: It centrally analyzes human eye-tracking measures for dyslexic versus typical reading, but naturalistic reading is not an interactive sequential-decision task under the rubric.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: It combines active working-memory stages with phase-lag and entropy-based EEG functional connectivity to distinguish healthy, MCI, and Alzheimer groups.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: Excluded because the reported evaluation consists of controlled simulations and synthetic test-item sampling without a human-participant study.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: It reviews EEG-based assessment of human attention and learning but does not itself implement any of the four target methodological elements.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: It uses frequency-specific steady-state auditory and visual EEG responses for human cognitive assessment, although the paradigms are passive and involve only five healthy volunteers.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: It uses interactive VR wayfinding with sequential navigation decisions, but the summary reports EEG classification without qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity methodology.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: Excluded because it is a theoretical neural-circuit study with simulated tasks and no human-participant population.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: It targets human cognitive-load biomarkers, but the auditory protocol is not clearly interactive and the EEG analysis is limited to individual band summaries and learned features.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: It predicts human executive-function scores from passive sleep EEG and ECG, using band-power features and deep classification rather than an analyzed task or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It is relevant to early dementia assessment but only reviews conventional clinical instruments and implements none of the target behavioral, neural, ocular, or computational methods.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: It addresses early cognitive-impairment detection with EEG, but the FPVS condition is explicitly passive and the summary provides only CNN classification rather than qualifying spectral or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: It evaluates interactive sequential working-memory and reaction-time tasks in VR and PC formats, but includes no eye tracking, qualifying EEG, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: Excluded because it benchmarks cognitive and executive abilities of LLMs rather than studying human participants.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: It validates interactive executive-function minigames in a clinical human sample and analyzes continuous movement and gaze trajectories as stable cognitive-style features.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: It records eye tracking and continuous behavior while human participants navigate VR mazes and perform attention and working-memory tasks.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: It centrally models human fixation sequences from panoramic eye-tracking datasets, but image viewing is passive and the Transformer is not a cognitive generative decision model.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: It measures human EEG and executive-function outcomes for stimulation safety, but no interactive paradigm or qualifying EEG frequency or connectivity analysis is specified.
